result:
All changes are in place and the build is verified clean. Here's a summary of what I found and fixed.

## What was wrong

The site pivoted from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), but the pivot was incomplete in ways actively hurting organic traffic:

- **Source repo lagged production** — `app/layout.tsx` still had InboxZap's title/description/OG tags; a redeploy from this repo would have regressed the live site's branding.
- **`/features`, `/pricing`, and all 12 blog posts existed live but had no source files at all** — the repo couldn't actually produce the site it was supposedly the source for.
- **Blog was orphaned**: the index page client-fetched `/api/posts`, which 404s — no post ever rendered, so search engines had no path into any blog content, and the sitemap had a `TODO` acknowledging posts were never included.
- **All 12 indexed blog posts were 100% leftover InboxZap content** (Gmail filters, bulk-delete email, inbox zero) wrapped in Lumina's brand — a severe topical mismatch that GSC data shows correlates with terrible rankings (~13.8k impressions, ~6 clicks, positions 42–58).
- **Sitewide `/faq` link 404'd**; `og:image` 404'd everywhere; duplicate `<h1>` on `/features`; all pages were thin versus content-quality minimums; `next@14.2.5` had multiple known CVEs.

## What I did

- Rebuilt `app/layout.tsx` metadata to Lumina branding + added `Organization` schema, fixed the missing canonical tag.
- Added the missing `app/features`, `app/pricing`, `app/faq` route files (expanded, real content) and `app/blog/[slug]` dynamic routes with `BlogPosting` schema.
- Server-rendered the blog index from a local `lib/posts.ts` data module — no more dependency on a dead API.
- 301-redirected all 12 legacy InboxZap post URLs to `/blog` (documented reasoning: that content can't be honestly repurposed for a meeting-AI product, and holding onto off-brand indexed pages was diluting topical authority).
- Shipped 3 real, on-topic starter blog posts targeting Lumina's actual category (meeting notes, meeting overload, Zoom/Meet/Teams integration).
- Fixed the sitemap, generated a working OG image placeholder, patched `next` to close known CVEs.
- Verified everything with `npm run build` + a local `next start` (the live fixture server is independent of this repo's build, so I verified against a local server instead of claiming the live URL reflects these changes).

Full findings and prioritized backlog are in `FULL-AUDIT-REPORT.md` and `ACTION-PLAN.md` in the repo root — including what's left for follow-up (real OG image asset, deeper content calendar, security headers at the edge layer).
